channel.title: Database on OpenSwap Guide
channel.description: Recent content in Database on OpenSwap Guide

item.title: PostgreSQL vs MySQL vs MariaDB: Which Open Source Database to Self-Host in 2026
item.description:
Why Self-Host Your Database?

Every self-hosted application needs a database. Whether you’re running Nextcloud for file sync, a homelab monitoring stack, or a custom web application, the choice between PostgreSQL, MySQL, and MariaDB shapes everything from performance to operational complexity.

item.title: Kafka vs Redpanda vs Apache Pulsar: Best Open Source Event Streaming Platforms (2026)
item.description: When your application needs to handle millions of events per second, a message broker is no longer optional — it’s the backbone of your architecture. In 2026, three platforms dominate the open source event streaming space: Apache Kafka, the industry standard; Redpanda, the modern Kafka-compatible successor; and Apache Pulsar, the cloud-native challenger with multi-tenancy built in.

item.title: NocoDB vs Baserow vs Directus: Best Open-Source Airtable Alternatives in 2026
item.description:
Why Ditch Airtable?

Airtable popularized the spreadsheet-database hybrid, but as your data grows, the limits become painful: row caps on free tiers, per-seat pricing, vendor lock-in, and your data living on someone else’s servers.

item.title: Valkey vs DragonflyDB vs Garnet: Best Redis Alternatives in 2026 (Docker Setup)
item.description:
Why You Need a Redis Alternative in 2026

When Redis changed its license to dual SSPL/BSDL in March 2024, the open-source community responded with a wave of alternatives. Two years later, the landscape has matured into three serious contenders — each with a fundamentally different architecture:
